title: Lesson 5: (Vocab) - ここ, そこ, あそこ...

intro_cards.title: Long Vowels in Hiragana
intro_cards.body:
In Japanese, holding a vowel sound longer can change the meaning of a word. In hiragana, long vowels are written by repeating or adding a vowel character:

いい (ii) — the い sound is held longer
ええ (ee) — the え sound is held longer

Common patterns you'll encounter:
あ-column: add あ (おかあさん, mother)
い-column: add い (おにいさん, older brother)
う-column: add う (すうじ, number)
え-column: usually add い (せんせい, teacher)
お-column: usually add う (おとうさん, father)

Katakana uses a simpler dash (ー) instead — you'll learn that later.

items.gloss: there (place relatively near listener); that place
